name: Cranberry Orange Pound Cake, Butter Rum Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Fresh or frozen cranberries; chopped (do not thaw)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Generously grease and lightly flour a 12-cup fluted tube pan; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat sugar and butter until light and fluffy. Add vanilla and orange rind. Beat in eggs one at a time, stirring well after each addition.
4. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Add the flour mixture to the egg batter alternately with sour cream, stirring well after each addition. Gently stir in cranberries; pour batter into prepared pan.
5. Bake until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 65-75 minutes. Cool in the pan for 15 minutes; invert cake onto a serving plate and remove from the pan.
6. While cooling, make the sauce. In a small saucepan, combine sugar and flour. Stir in half-and-half and butter. Cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly, stirring constantly. Remove from heat; stir in rum. Serve warm sauce over cake.
